comics.title: DHCP Friday
comics.alt: A sysadmin contemplates making a DHCP change on a Friday afternoon. Ten minutes later, every device loses connectivity. The sysadmin — now in sunglasses, watching 192.168.1.1 go DOWN — remains unbothered. He has simply moved beyond fear.

comics.title: Bring Your Own Jar
comics.alt: A person correctly predicts that any event requiring you to bring your own jar will be a good time. They attend Fermentation Fun Night. They were right.

comics.title: UUID Art Hash
comics.alt: UUIDs are technically correct but illegible to humans. A Pictographic Art Hash (PAH) maps any identifier to a short sequence of emoji — deterministic, compact, and scannable at a glance. The Cool Sysadmin closes the case: UUIDs for computers, art hashes for humans. Ship it.
